title: solanum tuberosum subsp. chiloense
intro: Solanum tuberosum subsp. chiloense, commonly known as the Chilean potato, is a sub-species of the Solanum tuberosum plant, which is a type of wild potato native to the Andean region of South America. The Chilean potato has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of the Chilean potato include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and improve digestive health. The herbal preparations of the Chilean potato can be made into teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
